prompt: A VitalPath Health Operations management system that includes offline-first APIs for member wellness tracking, controlled operational workflows, and auditable work handling for clinics and wellness programs running on an internal network. The backend, implemented in Rust using Actix-web with Diesel and PostgreSQL persistence, exposes Authentication/Session APIs for local username-and-password login, token-based sessions with 30-minute inactivity expiry, and role-based access for Administrator, Care Coach, Approver, and Member. Health Profile APIs support create/query/update of a member’s demographic and lifestyle profile (date of birth, sex enum, height in inches, weight in pounds, activity level enum, allergy/dietary restriction free-text capped at 1,000 chars) and time-series metric entries (weight, body fat %, waist/hip/chest inches) limited to one entry per member per metric per calendar day, with derived progress summaries returned by date range. Goal APIs allow multiple concurrent goals per member (fat loss, muscle gain, glucose control enums), each with a start date, optional target date, baseline, target value, and an “active/paused/completed” state; completion is auto-evaluated when the latest metric meets target for 7 consecutive days. Workflow Engine APIs allow Administrators to define approval templates by business type (e.g., profile correction, goal exception, data export), org unit, and amount/risk tier, supporting serial and parallel nodes; runtime workflow instances support submit, approve, reject, return-for-edit, withdraw (before first approval), and reassignment/additional sign-off, with SLA timers (default 48 business hours per node) generating internal tasks. Work-Order/State Machine APIs manage operational tickets (intake, triage, in-progress, waiting-on-member, resolved, closed) with allowed transitions, auto-routing by org unit and ticket type, processing notes, and closed-loop tracking. Data is modeled with normalized tables for users, roles, org_units, members, health_profiles (1:1), metric_types, metric_entries (indexed by member_id+metric_type+entry_date), goals, workflow_templates, workflow_nodes, workflow_instances, approvals, work_orders, notifications, notification_templates, deliveries, and audit_logs; primary identifiers use UUID, timestamps are stored in UTC, and unique constraints prevent duplicate usernames and duplicate daily metrics. Notification and Task Reminder Center APIs generate in-app messages and tasks only (no SMS/email/push), using template variables, per-user subscriptions, scheduled reminders (e.g., daily at 8:00 AM local org time) and event-triggered notices (SLA breach, return-for-edit), with send frequency caps of 3 per template per user per day, retry up to 5 times with exponential backoff, delivery logs, and read receipts. Analytics APIs provide aggregated metrics for program events (popularity, registration conversion, attendance, cancellations, channel/tag distributions) filterable by time range, org unit, and event attributes, and support offline exports to CSV/Excel files stored on the same host with export actions fully audited. Security and compliance requirements include strict input validation and SQL injection prevention via parameterized queries, CSRF/XSS protections appropriate for API token usage, HMAC-based API request signing for privileged integrations, rate limits of 60 requests/minute/user and 10 failed logins/15 minutes with lockout and local CAPTCHA challenge, encrypted-at-rest storage for sensitive fields (dietary restrictions, medical notes) using AES-256 with key rotation every 180 days, and masked display rules (e.g., show only last 2 characters of internal identifiers in logs). Full-chain audit trails must record login attempts, data changes, exports, and configuration edits with actor, timestamp, before/after hashes, and reason codes. The system runs fully offline in a single Docker deployment, supports daily encrypted backups to a locally mounted volume with 30-day retention and quarterly restore drills, and meets non-functional targets of p95 < 300 ms for typical queries under 50 concurrent users, with structured logs and health metrics exposed for local observability without external services.
